Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar May-03-2010 Continued To Jun-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Walter Youngman for Robin Brake.

Mr. Youngman stated that Robert Vietti had a pour over will, that the two Beneficiaries of the Trust were Robin and her Sister, that there was zero value as the asset was sold and that there was $300,000.00 in cash. In addition, he stated that they would have to open an estate for Robert in Contra Costa County, reported on the status of the Creditor's Claims and stated that he needed 45 days to close the estate. The matter was continued to June 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the Amended Petition for Final Distribution.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung and Deputy Neu.

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Apr-05-2010 Continued To May-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Substitution Of Attorney
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Walter Youngman for and with Robin Brake.

Mr. Youngman stated that he just received the file electonically from Mr. Woodlief late Friday, that he had not reviewed it and that the Substitution of Attorney had been filed. The Petition for Settlement of First and Final Distribution was continued to April 5, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Continued To Apr-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Continued To Mar-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief in Propria Persona. Walter Youngman personal Attorney for and with Robin Brake.

Mr. Woodlief stated that he was willing to substitute out as Counsel if necessary and that sanctions in the amount of $1,250.00 had been paid. Mr. Youngman stated that he would substitute in as Counsel and that he would finish the remaining acts necessary to close the estate. In addition, Mr. Youngman stated that Robert Vietti (Brother of James) had a revocable intervivos trust, that Robert's surviving Spouse was now deceased and that the assets were distributable to the two Daughters. Furthermore, Mr. Youngman stated that Mr. Sherlock (Business Partner of James) agreed that 50% of the proceeds they receive from the sale of the property and the liquor license goes to the Distributees. The petition for final distribution was continued to March 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of filing of the Substition of Attorney was also set for the same date and time. Sanctions against Ms. Brake are vacated.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Off Calendar

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Continued To Mar-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Walter Youngman for and with Robin Brake.

The Court stated that there was no appearance by Mr. Woodlief and that it was putting the matters over to March 8, 2010 to see if the Court could reach Mr. Woodlief. Mr. Youngman inquired whether there was any issue of sanctions as to his client. The Court requested Ms. Brake's telephone and address. Ms. Brake stated her mailing address: P.O. Box 805, Clayton, CA, 94517 and listed her phone number as set forth on the record. Mr. Youngman stated that he represented Ms. Brake in her personal capacity. The Court stated that it was not discharging Mr. Woodlief today, that it would discharge him on March 8th, and that Mr. Youngman was representing Ms. Brake now.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Continued To Mar-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Continued To Mar-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Continued To Mar-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Woodlief for Stephen Vietti.

Mr. Woodleif stated that he filed the second amended declaration with an attachment of the death certificate, that he did not have the 13100 Declaration and that clarification was provided as to the transfer of property. The Court informed Mr. Woodlief that sanctions in the amount of $1,250.00 have been imposed and will remain, that additional sanctions would be imposed if the 13100 declaration is not filed, and that if the register of actions did not show payment of said sanctions by February 18, 2010, then notice will be sent to the State Bar. The matter was continued to March 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Dec-21-2009 Continued To Feb-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Walter Youngman for Robin Brake.

Mr. Youngman stated that he was Ms. Brake's personal Attorney and that there was no contact with Mr. Woodlief. The Court noted that Miles Woodlief was not present. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Attorney Miles Woodlief for failure to to file the 13100 Declaration and for failure to submit a proposed order. The return date is February 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will commence as of today and stop when the 13100 Declaration is filed and the proposed order is submittted to the Court.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Continued To Dec-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for and with Robin Brake (Successor Administrator).

Mr. Woodlief provided an endorsed copy of a proof of service and stated that the Franchise Tax Board was given a notice of hearing in the packet he provided to them with the copy of the petitions. In addition, he stated that the only interest of the estate was a 50% interest in a corporation, that it is an LLC that holds real property in San Francisco, that the property was held in the corporation, that the estate was share holder of the LLC and that the estate itself does not hold the real property. Furthermore, he stated that the property was the Molotouf Bar on Haight St., that the cash value of the estate was less than $100,000.00, that he received a mix match of things with regard to Estate of Robert Vietti, that the other property was sold and that some of the money was distributed to the Creditors. The Court directed Counsel to comply with probate code 13100. The matter was continued to December 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the petition is not in a position to be adjudicated, additional sanctions would be imposed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Settlement Of First And Final Distribution.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for Robin Brake.

Mr. Woodlief stated that they identified problems with the real property, that they found that the property remained in posession of the corporaton, that it was in the process of being returned to the estate, that the estate owned 50% of the corporation and that he would be refiling the petition for final distribution. The matter was continued to October 19, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Maria Lertore, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Nov-19-2008 Set For Order To Show Cause On Dec-31-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth MacDonald specially appearing for Miles Woodlief.

The Court noted that Ms. Brake was not present. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Robin Brake (Successor Administrator) and Miles Woodlief (Attorney) to appear on December 31, 2008 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Guitron.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for Robin Brake.

Mr. Woodlief stated that he submitted the information to the Referee and that he was waiting to see if more information was required. The matter was continued to November 19, 2008 for status on filing of the Inventory and Appraisal. The Court informed Mr. Woodlief that sanctions would continue.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Wong.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Continued To Oct-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

There being no appearances, the Court ordered sanctions in the amount of $50.00 per day against Counsel and the Administrator for every day that the Final Inventory and Appraisal is not filed. The matter was continued to October 15, 2008 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-19-2008 Continued To Sep-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for the Successor Administrator.

Mr. Woodlief stated that he received additional information relating to the inventory, that he faxed it to the Probate Referee and requested a continuance. The matter was continued to September 24, 2008. The issue regarding sanctions will be addressed at that time.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Linda Forster CSR#13286. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-08-2008 Continued To Aug-06-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for the Successor Administrator.

Mr. Woodlief stated that the inventory was completed, that there was a partial appraisal from the referee and that some assets were not appraised as he was waiting for bank records. In addition, he stated that the issues raised in the Examiner's notes were resolved. The matter was continued to August 6, 2008. The Court will impose a $50.00 surcharge per day for everyday that Counsel does not comply with the filing of the proper Inventory and Appraisal.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Continued To Jul-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for the Administrator.

Mr. Woodlief stated that a partial inventory on the real property had been filed, that he had provided everything to the Referee and the Inventory and Appraisal should be filed within 30 days. The matter was continued to July 8, 2008. The Inventory and Appraisal should be filed by then. The Court will address the issue regarding sanctions on that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR#4609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for the Petitioner.

Mr. Woodlief requested a continuance stating that he had not received a return call from the Probate Referee. The matter was continued to May 20, 2008. The Court will not impose sanctions at this time.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: B. Mann.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miles Woodlief for Robin Blake (Administrator).

Mr. Woodlief stated that a referee needed to be appointed, that he would follow up with the referee today, that he would follow up with the accounting and move to close the estate. The matter was continued to May 7, 2008. The Inventory and Appraisal must be filed by then. If not filed, sanctions may be imposed for each day following the continued date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Mr. Woodlief reported that the letters, the request for referee, and the inventory had been filed. In addition, he stated that the new Administrator was very involved. The matter was continued to February 06, 2008 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.
